name: Housing Authority of Mingo County
description_short: HUD-affiliated housing authority serving low-income families, elderly, and disabled persons across five West Virginia counties with rental assistance and counseling programs.
description_long:
The Housing Authority of Mingo County is a government-affiliated housing agency established in 1977, operating in Delbarton, West Virginia. It serves low-income individuals, families, elderly residents, and persons with disabilities across Mingo, McDowell, Logan, Wayne, and Wyoming counties. The organization is governed by a five-member Board of Directors and operates as a HUD-authorized public housing authority.

The authority administers approximately 1,500 Housing Choice Voucher (HCV/Section 8) units and operates multiple housing assistance programs. Services include HCV/Section 8 voucher programs with income eligibility guidelines, emergency housing assistance, counseling services including USDA Rural Development loan products, multi-family housing programs, and fair housing resources. They also provide rental payment processing, fraud reporting mechanisms, and property maintenance services for their housing stock. Fair housing discrimination reporting and reasonable accommodations processes are available to residents.

As a government housing authority rather than a for-profit lender, the organization operates under HUD compliance requirements and focuses on expanding affordable housing access rather than generating revenue. They provide comprehensive support including family self-sufficiency programs, domestic violence protections under VAWA, fire safety education, lead hazard information, and disaster preparedness resources. Employment opportunities and procurement processes are publicly listed on their website.

The organization serves a primarily rural, low-income region of West Virginia. While they offer substantial housing assistance programs, availability of vouchers and services may be limited by funding constraints typical of public housing authorities. The counseling program appears focused on housing-related guidance rather than comprehensive financial counseling, and they operate within geographic limitations serving only five specific counties.
pros: HUD-approved and government-affiliated, ensuring regulatory compliance and legitimate oversight
Administers 1,500 HCV/Section 8 vouchers providing substantial rental assistance across five counties
Offers emergency housing assistance for immediate housing crises
Provides USDA Rural Development Direct Loan Program counseling (FAHE 502 loans)
Includes comprehensive resident protections including VAWA domestic violence provisions
Offers fair housing services with discrimination reporting and reasonable accommodations processes
Multi-family housing programs expand options beyond voucher-only assistance
cons: Limited geographic service area (only Mingo, McDowell, Logan, Wayne, and Wyoming counties in West Virginia)
Likely significant waitlists for HCV vouchers given rural demand and funding constraints
Counseling program appears narrowly focused on housing rather than comprehensive financial or credit counseling
Income eligibility requirements may exclude working families above poverty thresholds
No online application system visible; application process requires direct contact
best_for: Low-income renters in rural West Virginia seeking Section 8 or HCV voucher assistance
Elderly and disabled individuals needing affordable housing with accessibility accommodations
Families and individuals experiencing housing instability or emergency housing crises
Rural residents interested in USDA Rural Development financing and housing counseling
diagnosis: Best for low-income residents and families in rural West Virginia counties seeking affordable rental housing through government-backed voucher programs or emergency assistance. Primary caveat is limited geographic availability (five-county service area only) and likely significant voucher waitlists given rural funding constraints; applicants must meet strict income eligibility guidelines and contact the authority directly to apply.
services: HCV/Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program administration
USDA Rural Development Direct Loan Program (FAHE 502) counseling
Fair housing discrimination reporting and investigation
Reasonable accommodations requests and appeals process
Housing Quality Standards (HQS) inspections for landlords
Fire safety and lead hazard education
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Housing authority serving low-income families, elderly, and disabled persons across five West Virginia counties with rental assistance.
